seed_problem: Emit a full machine schedule for a synthetic JSSP instance; checker validates precedence/no-overlap and scores min(100, 100*optimum/makespan).
mutation: composite: generalize the classic job-shop to a FLEXIBLE job-shop (each task is eligible on one of several assets with asset-dependent durations, so the solver must choose BOTH the asset assignment AND the timing), and replace the intractable optimum in the score by a checker-computed always-feasible sequential fallback B = sum of first-listed durations, keeping scoring deterministic as ratio = min(1, 0.1*B/F).
title: Halley Station: Flexible Field-Work Schedule
strategies: sequential fallback: run every task on its first-listed asset back-to-back on one timeline (makespan = B)
job-order list scheduling: process jobs in order, appending each task to the eligible asset that yields the earliest completion (no gap insertion)
active-schedule dispatch with gap insertion + machine selection under multiple priority rules (SPT/LPT/MWKR/random) and seeded random restarts, keeping the best makespan
critical-path / local-search refinement reassigning bottleneck tasks off the slow asset into idle gaps
